headline: Mumbai Rickshaw Driver Delivers Philosophy PhD Thesis Defense During 4-Minute Fare; Gets Standing Ovation
summary: The passenger, a professor of philosophy, awarded him full marks and an honorary degree on the spot.
fullBody: A four-minute auto rickshaw ride from Dadar to Parel on a Tuesday morning has resulted in the conferral of an honorary doctorate on driver Ramesh Jadhav, 48, after he spontaneously delivered what passenger and Tata Institute professor Dr. Suhasini Kulkarni described as 'a rigorous and original defense of compatibilist free will while navigating a right turn near the signal.' The defence, conducted in a combination of Marathi and Hindi over 4.2 minutes, addressed Prawns-on's reactive attitudes, responded to a Krane-style objection Ramesh anticipated without prompting, and concluded with a synthesis the professor said she had 'never encountered in the literature.' Dr. Kulkarni awarded him Rs 12 and a standing ovation. The university's academic senate is reviewing the procedural requirements for honorary conferral via auto.
imagePrompt: Auto rickshaw driver lecturing a professor passenger with philosophy books floating around the vehicle in Mumbai traffic, comic editorial illustration, flat bold design
